articles.title: Scientists discover the brain’s hidden “stop scratching” switch
articles.description: Scientists have uncovered a hidden “stop-scratching” signal in the nervous system that tells your brain when enough scratching is enough. The discovery centers on a molecule called TRPV4, which acts like part of an internal braking system for itch relief. In experiments involving chronic itch similar to eczema, mice missing this signal scratched less often—but when they did scratch, they couldn’t stop.

articles.title: 2026 market turmoil? These 10 classic investing rules still hold the key
articles.description: Bob Farrell's investing rules remain relevant today. Markets revert to the mean and excesses lead to opposite extremes. There are no new eras, and sharp rises lead to sharp corrections. The crowd often gets it wrong at extremes, driven by fear and greed. Strong markets are broad-based, and bear markets have phases.

articles.title: Scientists stunned as volcano cloud destroys methane in the atmosphere
articles.description: A colossal underwater volcano in the South Pacific may have revealed a surprising new weapon against climate change. After the 2022 eruption of Hunga Tonga–Hunga Ha’apai, scientists detected enormous amounts of formaldehyde in the atmosphere — a telltale sign that methane, one of the planet’s most powerful greenhouse gases, was being destroyed. Researchers now believe volcanic ash mixed with salty seawater and sunlight created reactive chlorine particles that effectively “cleaned up” some of the methane released by the eruption itself.

articles.title: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship arrives in Tenerife
articles.description: The cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak arrived early Sunday off the Spanish island of Tenerife, where the process of sending most passengers back to their home countries will get underway.

articles.title: Scientists say a critical Atlantic ocean current is weakening and the world could feel the impact
articles.description: Scientists have uncovered strong evidence that a major Atlantic Ocean current system tied to global climate is weakening. The slowdown has been detected across a vast region of the North Atlantic over nearly two decades. Since this ocean circulation helps regulate weather and temperatures, changes could affect storms, rainfall, sea levels, and even winter conditions in parts of Europe and North America.

articles.title: Rabada leads Purple Cap race, Gill rises on Orange Cap table at IPL 2026

articles.title: Traders looking for next leg in global stocks rally bet on Asia
articles.description: Investors are increasingly betting on Asia for the next phase of the global equities rally, driven by enthusiasm around artificial intelligence and strong performances in South Korean and Taiwanese markets. Strategists remain bullish on AI-linked semiconductor and hardware stocks, while India lags due to oil dependence, weaker currency trends and limited AI exposure.

articles.title: Antarctica is melting from below and scientists say it’s worse than expected
articles.description: Scientists have uncovered a hidden Antarctic threat that could accelerate global sea level rise far faster than expected. Deep beneath floating ice shelves, long channels carved into the ice appear to trap warmer ocean water, dramatically speeding up melting from below. Even regions of East Antarctica once considered relatively stable may be far more vulnerable than scientists realized. Researchers warn that current climate models may be missing this dangerous process entirely, meaning future sea level rise could be underestimated.

Simplicity and consistency are key advantages for long-term success.

articles.title: Scientists reversed liver aging with young gut bacteria in stunning study
articles.description: Rebooting the gut microbiome with bacteria from youth may help stop aging-related liver damage and even prevent liver cancer, according to new research in mice. Older mice that received their own preserved youthful microbiome showed less inflammation, reduced DNA damage, and no signs of liver cancer. Researchers also found that the treatment suppressed a cancer-linked gene called MDM2, making older mice biologically resemble younger ones.

articles.title: 8,500 steps a day can help dieters keep weight off, research suggests
articles.description: New research presented at the European Congress on Obesity (ECO 2026) in Istanbul, Turkey (12–15 May) and published in the International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health shows that doing around 8,500 steps a day can help people keep weight off after dieting.

articles.title: Over half of parents in England are pestered by their children to buy junk food while food shopping, survey suggests
articles.description: A study using a nationally representative survey of parents in England, presented at the European Congress on Obesity (ECO 2026, Istanbul, Turkey, 12–15 May), reveals that over half (58%) of parents are frequently pestered by their children or teens to purchase products high in fats, salts and/or sugar (HFSS) when food shopping in stores or online, and almost three-quarters (72%) reported often buying the requested item.

articles.title: Study shows benefits in obesity-linked conditions of losing more weight with GLP-1 treatment
articles.description: New research presented at the European Congress on Obesity (ECO 2026, Istanbul, Turkey, 12–15 May) shows that, following treatment for obesity or diabetes or both, using incretin-based drugs, losing more weight versus less weight, and losing a little weight versus gaining weight, both lead to a relatively lower risk of obesity-related conditions. The study is by Professor John Wilding of the University of Liverpool, UK, and colleagues.

articles.title: Himanta Biswa Sarma set to be Assam chief minister — only non-Congress CM to assume office two times in a row
articles.description: The new government will take oath on Tuesday, 12 May at the Khanapara veterinary college in the presence of the PM, Union ministers and other top diginitaries.

articles.title: Mythos | Double-edged sword
articles.description: The Anthropic AI model can not only identify vulnerabilities in cyberspace that humans may have missed, but also generate ways to exploit them, potentially even for non-experts

articles.title: AAP holds protests against BJP over ED action against Punjab Minister Sanjeev Arora
articles.description: The AAP leaders and volunteers burnt effigies of the BJP-led Centre and raised slogans against it. They were also carrying placards which read 'ED-BJP gathjod murdabad'

articles.title: Stalin reacts to Tamil Nadu CM Vijay speech: ‘Money is there; what is required is efficient administration’
articles.description: Responding to Mr. Vijay’s claim that his electoral promises were practical, Mr. Stalin said the new Chief Minister had only just entered administration and would now learn the challenges involved in fulfilling promises.

articles.title: 'Money’s all there': Stalin reacts to Vijay’s first speech, says Tamil Nadu debt 'well within permissible limits'
articles.description: Tamil Nadu's new Chief Minister Vijay claimed the previous government left a Rs 10 lakh crore debt and an empty treasury. However, former CM M.K. Stalin refuted this, stating the state's debt is within limits and urging Vijay not to start his tenure with such claims. Vijay vowed transparency and prioritizing public welfare.

articles.title: Armani May Split Stake For Sale to Follow Will, Repubblica Says
articles.description: The Armani Group may split the 15% stake that is up for sale equally among its preferred buyers, as the company moves closer to implementing the wishes of founder Giorgio Armani, La Repubblica reported on Sunday.

topStories.title: US Awaits Iran Reply as Aramco Says Hormuz Opening No Quick Fix
topStories.description: The wait stretched on for Iran’s response to a US proposal to end 10 weeks of war, as the world’s largest oil company warned it would take months for the market to return to normal even if the Strait of Hormuz reopened immediately.

topStories.description: The cruise ship linked to a deadly hantavirus outbreak arrived off Tenerife, where Spanish health authorities plan to test and evacuate passengers to their home countries.

topStories.description: Beverly Hills detectives try to figure out how a widow ended up dead below a staircase in her mansion. "48 Hours" correspondent Erin Moriarty reports.

topStories.description: The MV Hondius cruise ship, on which the hantavirus infection was identified, is making its way to the Canary Islands.

topStories.title: Kristin Smart search concludes at San Luis Obispo County home with no remains found
topStories.description: The San Luis Obispo County Sheriff's Office has concluded its latest search connected to the disappearance of Kristin Smart​ at a property in Arroyo Grande, but officials say her remains were not recovered.

topStories.title: Cruise ship stricken by hantavirus reaches Canary Islands before planned evacuations
topStories.description: Health officials will begin the complex process of evacuating the passengers and most of the crew, and repatriating them to their respective countries.

topStories.title: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship arrives in Tenerife
topStories.description: The cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak arrived early Sunday off the Spanish island of Tenerife, where the process of sending most passengers back to their home countries will get underway.

topStories.title: Scientists say a critical Atlantic ocean current is weakening and the world could feel the impact
topStories.description: Scientists have uncovered strong evidence that a major Atlantic Ocean current system tied to global climate is weakening. The slowdown has been detected across a vast region of the North Atlantic over nearly two decades. Since this ocean circulation helps regulate weather and temperatures, changes could affect storms, rainfall, sea levels, and even winter conditions in parts of Europe and North America.

topStories.title: Hantavirus: American oncologist who became cruise ship's de facto head doctor speaks
topStories.description: After the MV Hondius' doctor contracted the hantavirus, one American doctor onboard jumped into action to help passengers.

topStories.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

dailyNews.description: North Korea's revised constitution reportedly requires an automatic nuclear strike if Kim Jong Un is killed, according to intelligence briefings.

dailyNews.title: Suspected boat explosion injures 11 in Miami
dailyNews.description: Officials are investigating what caused the explosion at a popular marina in southern Florida.

dailyNews.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
dailyNews.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

dailyNews.title: How did a wealthy widow wind up dead below a staircase in her mansion?
dailyNews.description: Beverly Hills detectives responding to the death of 67-year-old Violet Yacobi — who was found on the marble floor below a staircase in her mansion — suspected foul play and her dentist son, and wondered if the family fortune was a motive for murder.

dailyNews.title: Wings rookie Azzi Fudd sets dubious WNBA record with lowest-scoring debut by top pick
dailyNews.description: UConn star Azzi Fudd set a WNBA record for the fewest points scored in a No. 1 pick's debut, managing just three points for the Dallas Wings.

dailyNews.title: Harris accuses Trump allies of trying to ‘rig’ 2026 midterms after Virginia court tosses redistricting measure
dailyNews.description: Kamala Harris claims Virginia Supreme Court ruling on redistricting maps boosts Trump's alleged effort to rig the 2026 midterm elections for Republicans.

dailyNews.title: Man accused of threatening former Prince Andrew near Sandringham home pleads not guilty in court
dailyNews.description: A 39-year-old man pleaded not guilty to threatening former Prince Andrew near his new home on the royal family's Sandringham estate in Norfolk.

dailyNews.title: Labour MP puts Cabinet 'on notice,' threatens to trigger leadership challenge against Starmer by Monday
dailyNews.description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces a potential Labour leadership challenge after the party suffered major losses in Thursday's local elections.

dailyNews.title: New Russian-Flagged LNG Tanker Appears to Load US-Sanctioned Gas
dailyNews.description: A liquefied natural gas tanker that recently switched flags to Russia appears to be loading fuel from a US-sanctioned project, marking Moscow’s latest move to expand its dark fleet designed to circumvent Western restrictions.

dailyNews.title: Atlanta Braves Hall of Fame manager Bobby Cox has died at 84
dailyNews.description: Cox managed Braves' teams that ruled National League and won 1995 World Series.

dailyNews.title: Redistricting Ruling in Virginia Adds to Gov. Abigail Spanberger’s Headaches
dailyNews.description: Gov. Abigail Spanberger had initially been skeptical of the push for new maps, but as the state’s leading Democrat she had a political stake in its fate.

dailyNews.title: Dramatic video shows moment Frontier Airlines flight hits trespasser on runway
dailyNews.description: A Frontier Airlines plane aborted take off after it hit and killed a trespasser who gained access to the runway. Video from inside the plane shows panicked passengers as smoke filled the cabin. Emergency responders reported that there appeared to be “human remains on the runway.” It is unclear how the individual made it on the tarmac. NBC News’ Liz Kreutz has the latest.

topStories.description: The Trump administration’s focus on the show is testament to the enduring influence of an old-fashioned broadcast TV program started 29 years ago.

topStories.description: The president has reworked his tariffs repeatedly — sometimes because they have been declared illegal — with more updates still to come.

topStories.description: We are entering a more perilous era where China no longer sees U.S. strength as something to fear.

topStories.description: President Trump said Saturday he "expects to hear very soon" from the Iranians.

topStories.title: Trump overseeing D.C. landmark overhauls in wartime
topStories.description: President Trump is overseeing an overhaul of some of D.C.'s most treasured landmarks and this week, shot back when asked why he'd focus on these projects during a war. Aaron Navarro reports.

topStories.title: U.S. still awaits Iranian response to peace plan, Trump says it could come "any minute"
topStories.description: The U.S. still has no reply from Iran on the latest peace plan, but President Trump says it could happen "any minute." But there is movement on wartime fuel prices, averaging $4.53 a gallon, up 25 cents for the second week. Holly Williams has more.

topStories.title: Iran focus at Trump-Xi summit may delay progress on tariffs, rare earths
topStories.description: Thanks to the Iran war, big issues for U.S. businesses like rare earths and supply chains may take a back seat on the agenda for the Trump-Xi summit.

dailyNews.description: FDA Commissioner Marty Makary has faced criticism over his leadership of the agency that oversees vaccine and drug approvals.

dailyNews.description: The secretary of state held warm meetings with Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and Pope Leo XIV after the president’s repeated criticism of both leaders.

dailyNews.description: The president expressed optimism about reaching a deal despite the attacks. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the U.S. is expecting a response from Iran on Friday.
